title: Add location toggle and user mode switching

requirements.text: Add a location enable/disable toggle to the Profile page that persists the `locationEnabled` boolean state (defaulting to `true`) in localStorage and reflects the current permission status. When disabled, suppress any location-dependent features (e.g., nearby buddies map, presence broadcasting). Sync this state with the existing `useLocationPermission` hook and the `LocationPermissionModal` component.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile page shows a visible toggle switch labeled 'Location' that reflects the current enabled/disabled state.
Toggling off hides or disables location-dependent UI (e.g., map nearby buddies, Go Live button on MapPage).
The `locationEnabled` state persists across page reloads via localStorage.
When toggling on from a disabled state, the LocationPermissionModal is shown to re-request browser permission.
State defaults to `true` on first load if no localStorage value exists.

requirements.text: Add a `userMode` state (defaulting to `"normal"`) to the SportContext and expose a setter so any page can switch modes. Define at least two modes: `"normal"` and `"buddy_finder"`. Display the current mode in the Profile page with a segmented control or dropdown to switch between modes. Persist the selected mode in localStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: SportContext exposes `userMode` (string) and `setUserMode` to all consumers.
Profile page renders a mode selector (segmented control or dropdown) showing at least 'Normal' and 'Buddy Finder' options.
Selecting a mode updates `userMode` in context and persists the value in localStorage.
The selected mode is restored from localStorage on app load.
Other pages that consume SportContext can read `userMode` to conditionally adjust behavior (e.g., MapPage highlights buddy-finder-specific UI when mode is `"buddy_finder"`).

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All state persistence must use localStorage only — no backend changes required.
userMode and locationEnabled must be managed in or alongside SportContext (frontend/src/context/SportContext.tsx).
nonGoals: Backend changes for location or user mode storage.
Real GPS geolocation implementation beyond what useLocationPermission already provides.
Adding new user modes beyond 'normal' and 'buddy_finder'.
